text: This is a robotic left lower lobe superior segmentectomy on a 70 year old patient with a 1.8 centimeter tumor of the left lower lobe superior segment.
The dissection is started by mobilizing the inferior pulmonary ligament to the level of the inferior pulmonary vein.
Here you can see a level 9 lymph node which has been removed and sent for pathology.
The ligament is taken down to the level of the inferior pulmonary vein, and again, we encounter another lymph node here.
The lung is then folded medially to expose a posterior hilum, where the posterior pleura is completely opened, and posterior hilum lymph nodes are removed.
The lung is then retracted down towards the diaphragm to expose the AP window space where level 5 and level 6 lymph nodes are removed.
We next turn our attention back down to the inferior pulmonary vein, where the superior segmental branch of the pulmonary vein will be dissected out.
Again, it is important to dissect out onto the periphery of the lung to better expose the segmental branches.
Here, the superior segmental vein is transected with a vascular load.
Cleaning of all these attachments will further facilitate the anatomy and dissection later on in the case.
This is a superior segmental bronchus, which is coming into view.
And removing this lymph node will allow us to get around the bronchus.
We next turn our attention to the fissure
where the superior segmental branch of the pulmonary artery will be dissected out as shown here.
And once completely rounded, a stapler is used to transect the artery.
A large lymph node seen here is then removed and in doing so will expose the bronchus to the superior segment.
The key here again is to dissect onto the parenchyma as distal as possible so that the anatomy becomes more clear.
This here is a superior segmental bronchus, which is then transected after test inflation.
ICG is given and Firefly is activated to help delineate the segment.
And here you can see the delineation of the basilar segment and the superior segment.
Once the segment has been demarcated,
Multiple green loads of the stapler are used to essentially staple off the superior segment.
The specimen is placed in an endo-catch bag and removed.
This patient did well post-operatively and was sent home on post-operative day number one.
Here the lung is allowed to re-expand
